detaileddescription.para: system directory path manager Generated from class MR::SystemPath. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).
Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SystemPath_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_SystemPath_DestroyArray(). Use MR_SystemPath_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_SystemPath_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Generated from constructor MR::SystemPath::SystemPath. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SystemPath_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_SystemPath. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_SystemPath. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::SystemPath::operator=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in this object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it had previously.

get the directory path for specified category Generated from method MR::SystemPath::getDirectory. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_filesystem_path_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
override the directory path for specified category, useful for custom configurations Generated from method MR::SystemPath::overrideDirectory. Parameter path is a UTF-8 encoded filesystem path.

get the resource files' directory path Generated from method MR::SystemPath::getResourcesDirectory. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_filesystem_path_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

get the font files' directory path Generated from method MR::SystemPath::getFontsDirectory. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_filesystem_path_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

get the plugin binaries' directory path Generated from method MR::SystemPath::getPluginsDirectory. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_filesystem_path_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

get the Python modules' binaries' directory path Generated from method MR::SystemPath::getPythonModulesDirectory. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_std_filesystem_path_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
get name all system fonts that have italics, bold, bold italics Generated from method MR::SystemPath::getSystemFonts. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.
